title: Kids Rule the World Day!
story: Every June 1, children around the globe get their own special day called International Children's Day! Schools throw parties with games, music, and treats. Families plan fun outings to parks, zoos, or museums just for kids. Many countries give children small gifts or organize parades where kids march with colorful banners. Some places even let children take over grown-up jobs for a few hours, like being mayor or news reporter! Social media fills with photos of smiling kids enjoying their day. It's a time to celebrate how amazing children are and remind everyone that kids deserve love, safety, and the chance to play and learn.
activity_question: What might children get to do on International Children's Day in some places?
activity_choices: Take over grown-up jobs like being mayor
activity_answer: Take over grown-up jobs like being mayor
bonus_fact: Over 120 countries celebrate this special day for children!

title: Moo-nificent World Milk Day!
story: Every June 1, people around the world celebrate World Milk Day by raising their glasses to this creamy white drink! Schools serve extra milk with lunch, dairy farms open their gates for tours, and families try new milk recipes like smoothies and milkshakes. Some people even dress up like cows or farmers to show their love for this nutritious beverage that helps build strong bones and teeth.
activity_question: What do some people dress up as on World Milk Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to drink milk - plain, chocolate, or in a smoothie?

title: Parents Get Their Perfect Day
story: Parents around the world get celebrated on June 1 during Global Day of Parents! Kids make handmade cards, cook breakfast in bed, and give extra hugs to show appreciation. Families post sweet photos together on social media and share stories about why their parents are amazing.
activity_question: What do kids often make for their parents on this special day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you could surprise your parents?

title: Polish Your Style on National Nail Polish Day
story: Tiny bottles of colorful magic take center stage every June 1 on National Nail Polish Day! People celebrate by painting their nails in wild colors, trying crazy designs, or even attempting nail art masterpieces. Some folks visit nail salons for professional manicures, while others host nail painting parties at home with friends. Social media fills up with photos of glittery fingers and toes showing off everything from rainbow stripes to tiny painted flowers. It's the perfect day to let your creativity shine right at your fingertips!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Nail Polish Day?
follow_up_question: What creative nail art design would you want to try?

title: Say Something Nice Day Spreads Smiles
story: Every June 1, people around the world celebrate Say Something Nice Day by sharing compliments and kind words with everyone they meet. Friends text sweet messages to each other, kids write thank-you notes to their teachers, and families take turns saying what they love about one another. Some people challenge themselves to give at least five genuine compliments throughout the day. Social media fills up with positive posts and encouraging comments. The holiday reminds us that a simple "you're awesome" or "thanks for being you" can completely brighten someone's day and costs absolutely nothing to give.
activity_question: What do people challenge themselves to do on Say Something Nice Day?
activity_choices: Give at least five genuine compliments
activity_answer: Give at least five genuine compliments
follow_up_question: What's the nicest compliment you've ever received that you still remember?

title: Kick Off Your Shoes Day!
story: Your feet are about to get their favorite holiday! National Go Barefoot Day happens every June 1, when people everywhere ditch their shoes and socks to feel grass, sand, and cool floors under their toes. Kids love running through sprinklers barefoot, while adults rediscover how amazing it feels to walk on different textures. Some people even go barefoot to work or school if it's allowed!
activity_question: What do people do on National Go Barefoot Day?
activity_choices: Take off their shoes and socks
activity_answer: Take off their shoes and socks
bonus_fact: Walking barefoot can help strengthen the tiny muscles in your feet!

title: Diving Deep for World Reef Day
story: Coral reefs get their own special celebration on June 1 with World Reef Day! People around the globe dive into action to protect these underwater rainbow cities. Marine biologists share amazing photos of colorful fish and coral on social media. Schools organize beach cleanups and reef education programs. Aquariums host special tours showing off their coral exhibits. Some people even "adopt" a piece of reef by donating money to conservation groups. It's a day when everyone becomes an ocean superhero, working together to keep these incredible underwater worlds healthy and bright for future generations to explore and enjoy.
activity_question: What do people do on World Reef Day to help protect coral reefs?
activity_choices: Organize beach cleanups and education programs
activity_answer: Organize beach cleanups and education programs
bonus_fact: Coral reefs are home to about 25% of all ocean fish species!
